---
title: "Approach"
---

# Analytical Approach

My approach combines analytical rigor, data structuring, and actionable insight generation.

I primarily work with complex, heterogeneous, or poorly structured data, using exploration, modeling, and interpretation methods.

The objective is consistent: transforming data into clear and decision-oriented insights.

---

## 1. Data exploration and understanding

- Exploratory data analysis (EDA) using R / Python  
- Identification of structures, anomalies, and biases  
- Text data analysis (qualitative and quantitative)  
- Integration of heterogeneous data sources  

This phase focuses on understanding the internal logic of the data before modeling.

---

## 2. Data cleaning and structuring

- Data cleaning, normalization, and disambiguation  
- Structuring complex datasets (tabular and relational)  
- Database modeling (SQL, Neo4j / Cypher)  
- Building usable datasets  

I place strong emphasis on handling messy or incomplete data requiring advanced structuring.

---

## 3. Statistical analysis and modeling

- Descriptive and inferential statistics  
- Modeling relationships between variables (regression, PCA, etc.)  
- Network and relational analysis  
- Critical interpretation of results  

My approach is grounded in solid statistical and econometric foundations (including Wooldridge).

---

## 4. Visualization and interpretation

- Data visualization (R, ggplot2, BI tools in progress)  
- Identification of patterns and relationships  
- Clear and structured representations  

Visualization is central to both analysis and communication.

---

## 5. Data storytelling and communication

- Translating analysis into actionable insights  
- Structuring results into clear narratives  
- Adapting communication to non-technical stakeholders  
- Concise and effective presentation  

I view analysis as a bridge between data and teams, supporting informed decision-making.

---

## 6. Tools and environment

- R (tidyverse, ggplot2)  
- Python (pandas, numpy)  
- SQL (in progress)  
- Neo4j / Cypher  
- Excel (advanced)  
- Power BI (in progress), Tableau (in progress)  
- RStudio / Posit, Jupyter, VS Code  

---

## 7. Key strengths

- Handling complex and poorly structured data  
- Text data analysis (qualitative & quantitative)  
- Advanced data structuring  
- Strong methodological rigor  
- Ability to communicate insights clearly  
- Strong autonomy in analytical work and learning  
- Rapid ability to adopt new tools, languages, and methods  

My positioning combines:  
> rigorous analysis → structuring → insight → communication.
